ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Customer's date of birth */,
  "loyalty_program_id" INTEGER /* ID of the loyalty program the customer is part of */,
  "subscription_status" INTEGER /* Status of the customer's subscription to newsletters/promotions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., purchase, inquiry, feedback) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the interaction */,
  "channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the interaction occurred */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer making the purchase */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the purchase */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount of the purchase */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used for the purchase */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product purchased */,
  "product_description" TEXT /* Description of the product purchased */,
  "discount_applied" REAL /* Any discount applied to the purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who submitted the feedback */,
  "submission_date" TEXT /* Date of feedback submission */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer (1-5) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments provided by the customer */,
  "feedback_category" TEXT /* Category of the feedback */,
  "follow_up_required" INTEGER /* Whether follow-up action is required */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user account */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user account */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, support, sales) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "criteria" TEXT /* Criteria used for segmenting customers (e.g., demographics, purchase history) */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the segment criteria */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment targeted by the campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "campaign_budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "expected_outcome" TEXT /* Expected outcome of the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_campaigns_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "support_tickets" (
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each support ticket */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who submitted the ticket */,
  "submission_date" TEXT /* Date the support ticket was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the ticket (e.g., open, closed, pending) */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution details for the ticket */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the ticket */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* User account assigned to resolve the ticket */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_support_tickets_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES user_accounts ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_support_tickets_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer receiving the notification */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign related to the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., delivered, failed) */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., promotional, transactional) */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Whether the customer has read the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES marketing_campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of permissions granted to this role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
